It makes sense that if people who in the past have disagreed agree about something, it's probably true. I wonder if there's some emergent thing that could challenge Wikipedia, which is more like permanent articles.
turns.zh: 这很迷人，因为有一群存在分歧的人，他们通过这种分歧过程合作，提供背景信息。这说得通，如果过去有分歧的人对某事达成一致，那很可能是真的。我想知道是否会有某种涌现的东西挑战维基百科，维基百科更像是永久性的文章。

turns.en: Wikipedia is very hierarchical, whereas Community notes is inherently not. There is no hierarchy. The acid test is that I can't change a Community note if somebody put a gun to my head. Community notes has all code open source, 100%, and all data open source, 100%. You can completely recreate any note in the system independently. If there was any interference, you'd notice immediately.
turns.zh: 维基百科是非常等级制的，而社区笔记本质上不是。没有等级制度。终极考验是，如果有人拿枪指着我的头，我也无法更改一条社区笔记。社区笔记的所有代码都是 100% 开源的，所有数据也是 100% 开源的。你可以独立地完全重建系统中的任何笔记。如果有任何干扰，你会立即注意到。

turns.en: By the way, on the topic of Grok, it's actually pretty good at criticizing you, which is great. I asked what has Elon Musk been wrong about. It says mostly lists rigorously all the times you predicted or promised when something will be released and it wasn't. The opener is really nice: 'Elon Musk, the man who has been wrong about more things than a wrong American in a wrong factory.' Then it goes on to list. That's pretty funny.
turns.zh: 顺便说一句，关于 Grok 的话题，它实际上很擅长批评你，这很棒。我问了 Elon Musk 在哪些事情上错了。它基本上严格列出了所有你预测或承诺某件事会发布但实际没有发布的情况。开头语很妙：‘Elon Musk，一个在错误工厂里比错误美国人还错得更多的人。’然后它继续列出。这很有趣。

turns.en: Can I see it? I haven't seen this before. This is cool.
turns.zh: 我能看看吗？我以前没见过这个。这很酷。

turns.en: Sure. It says: 'What has Elon Musk been wrong about? Well, where do I begin? First off, let's talk about the infamous funding secured tweet. This is different than what it gave me before, which was a bullet point list. Here's more narrative structure. Cyber truck unveiling where he supposedly unbreakable windows...'
turns.zh: 当然。它说：‘Elon Musk 在哪些事情上错了？嗯，从何说起呢？首先，让我们谈谈臭名昭著的“资金已到位”推文。这和我之前得到的不一样，之前是一个要点列表。这里更有叙事结构。Cybertruck 发布会，他声称牢不可破的窗户……’

turns.en: Which part is not correct?
turns.zh: 哪部分不正确？

turns.en: The first part. I did actually have the funding secured. There was a big civil trial in San Francisco, and the jury found me not guilty, unanimous finding. Here it's kind of implying that it was not in fact secured. I think this is taking things from the press. That is not correct. The reason I agreed to the fine for the SEC is not because the SEC was correct. That was extremely bad behavior by the SEC, corruption frankly. If I did not agree to pay the fine, Tesla would have gone bankrupt immediately. I was told by our CFO that the banks would immediately suspend our lines of credit, and if they did, we would have gone bankrupt instantly. So there would never have been an opportunity for a trial. It would be dead. It was like someone holding a gun to your kid's head and saying pay $20 million and admit this. Like a hostage negotiation. Was that story fully told? The SEC in its best form could be a force for good, it should be, but not once did the SEC go after any of the hedge funds who were non-stop shorting and distorting Tesla. Not once. They would lie flat out on TV for their own gain at the expense of retail investors. Not once, literally a thousand times, not once did the SEC pursue them. How do you explain this failure? The incentive structure is messed up. The lawyers of the SEC are not paid well; it's a fairly low paying job. But what they're looking for is a trophy from the SEC, something they put on their LinkedIn, from which they can get a job at a high-paying law firm. That's exactly what the lawyer here did. The reason they don't attack the hedge funds is because those hedge funds employ those law firms, and they know if they attack the hedge funds, they're affecting their future career prospects. So they sell small investors down the river for their own career. That's what actually happens. Regulatory capture. Not good. So the only reason I accepted the thing technically was a 'neither admit nor deny guilt' settlement. But the only reason I agreed to that at all was because I was told Tesla would be bankrupt otherwise. If there was an SEC investigation like this, banks would suspend funding, we'd be bankrupt immediately. Now we're in a much stronger position.
turns.zh: 第一部分。我确实有资金到位。在旧金山有一场大型民事审判，陪审团一致裁定我无罪。这里暗示实际上资金并未到位。我认为这是从媒体那里来的。那是不正确的。我同意向 SEC 支付罚款的原因，并不是因为 SEC 是正确的。那是 SEC 极其恶劣的行为，坦率地说是腐败。如果我不同意支付罚款，特斯拉会立即破产。我们的 CFO 告诉我，银行会立即暂停我们的信贷额度，如果那样，我们会立刻破产。所以永远不会有审判的机会。会直接死掉。这就像有人拿枪指着你孩子的头说，支付 2000 万美元并承认这一点。就像人质谈判。这个故事被完整讲述了吗？SEC 最好的形式可以成为一股向善的力量，它应该如此，但 SEC 从未追查过任何持续做空并扭曲特斯拉的对冲基金。一次也没有。他们为了自己的利益在电视上公然撒谎，牺牲散户投资者。一次也没有，实际上上千次，SEC 从未追查过他们。你如何解释这种失败？激励机制被破坏了。SEC 的律师薪水不高；这是一份相当低薪的工作。但他们想要的是从 SEC 获得一个奖杯，放在他们的 LinkedIn 上，然后他们可以凭此在高薪律师事务所找到工作。这正是这里的律师所做的。他们不攻击对冲基金的原因，是因为那些对冲基金雇佣了那些律师事务所，他们知道如果攻击对冲基金，会影响他们未来的职业前景。所以他们为了自己的职业生涯牺牲了小投资者。这就是实际发生的事情。监管俘获。不好。所以我接受这件事的唯一原因，技术上是一个‘既不承认也不否认有罪’的和解。但我同意它的唯一原因，是因为我被告知否则特斯拉会破产。如果 SEC 进行这样的调查，银行会暂停资金，我们会立即破产。现在我们处于一个更强大的位置。

turns.en: Take that, Grok.
turns.zh: 听到了吧，Grok。

turns.en: Yes, unfortunately Grok is taking too much from the conventional media. Also, that guy was not a cave diver. There's a time where Elon called the British cave diver a 'pedo guy' after the diver criticized Musk's plan to rescue a group of boys trapped in a Thai cave. That little slur earned him another lawsuit and he had to apologize and pay a settlement. That is false. There was no settlement.
turns.zh: 是的，不幸的是，Grok 从传统媒体那里获取了太多信息。另外，那个人不是洞穴潜水员。有一次，Elon 在一位洞穴潜水员批评他救援被困泰国洞穴男孩的计划后，称他为‘恋童癖’。那个小小的侮辱让他又吃了一场官司，他不得不道歉并支付和解金。那是假的。没有和解。

turns.en: There was a court case where a guy who is not a cave diver and was not part of the rescue team filed a lawsuit against me and lost. He received nothing. So in this case, it is wrong. It is also, I guess, taking this from the conventional media. Actually, there's an interesting question here. These are public court cases. Both the SEC civil case, where the civil complaints on the SEC guys lost, unanimous jury verdict in San Francisco. They picked San Francisco because they thought it was the place I was most likely to lose, and a unanimous verdict in my favor. The LA trial was also, they picked that venue because they thought I was most likely to lose, unanimous verdict in my favor. Both cases, I won.
turns.zh: 有个案子，一个不是洞穴潜水员、也不属于救援队的人起诉我，结果输了，一分钱都没拿到。所以这个案子是错的。而且，我想这也是从传统媒体那里来的。实际上，这里有个有趣的问题。这些都是公开的法庭案件。美国证券交易委员会的民事案，民事起诉方输了，旧金山陪审团一致裁决。他们选旧金山是因为觉得那里我最可能输，结果一致裁决对我有利。洛杉矶的审判也是，他们选那个地点是因为觉得我最可能输，又是一致裁决对我有利。两个案子我都赢了。

turns.en: Yeah, there's an interesting question here. There seems to be a lot more clicks if a journalistic organization writes a negative article about you, Elon Musk. That's like one of the best ways to get clicks. So how do you, if you're training Grok, not train on articles that have misaligned incentives? We need to add the training set of the actual legal decisions.
turns.zh: 对，这里有个有趣的问题。如果新闻机构写一篇关于你埃隆·马斯克的负面文章，似乎能获得更多点击量。这简直是获取点击的最佳方式之一。那么，如果你在训练Grok，如何避免训练那些激励错位的文章？我们需要把实际法律判决加入训练集。

turns.en: If so, that is a note. This is actually helpful, because if you actually read the court conclusions, which are public, they're completely the opposite of what the media wrote. So always striving for the ground truth. Yeah, beyond the judge actually write, what do the jury and the judge actually conclude? And in both cases, they found me innocent. And that's after the jury show for trying to find the venue where I'm most likely to lose.
turns.zh: 如果是这样，那是个提示。这实际上很有帮助，因为如果你真的去读法庭结论——这些是公开的——它们和媒体写的完全相反。所以总是追求事实真相。对，除了法官实际写的，陪审团和法官到底得出了什么结论？两个案子都判我无罪。而且那是在陪审团试图找我最可能输的地点之后。

turns.en: No, I mean, this is obviously can be a much greater critique than this. I mean, I've been far too optimistic about Autopilot. That was the critique I got, by the way, was more about that. Which is, it for each broke down a nice bullet point list for each of your companies, the set of predictions that you made when you will deliver, when you'll be able to solve for example self-driving, and gives you like a list, and it kind of compelling. And the basic takeaway is like you're often too optimistic about how long it takes to get something done.
turns.zh: 不，我的意思是，这显然可以是一个更严厉的批评。我的意思是，我对Autopilot过于乐观了。顺便说一句，我得到的批评更多是关于那个。那就是，它为你每个公司列出了漂亮的要点清单，你做出的预测，什么时候能交付，什么时候能解决比如自动驾驶，然后给你一个清单，还挺有说服力的。基本结论就是你经常对完成某事所需的时间过于乐观。

turns.en: Yeah, I mean, I would say that I'm pathologically optimistic on schedule. This is true. But while I am sometimes late, I always deliver in the end. Except with Uber Lilith? No, we'll see.
turns.zh: 是的，我是说，我会说我在时间表上病态地乐观。这是真的。但虽然我有时会迟到，我最终总能交付。除了Uber Lilith？不，我们等着瞧。

transcript.secZh: 对时间表与自动驾驶的乐观

transcript.sec: Political Involvement and Woke Mind Virus

turns.en: Okay. Is there, over the past year or so since purchasing X, you've become more political. Is there a part of you that regrets that?
turns.zh: 好的。在过去一年左右，自从收购X以来，你变得更加政治化。你有没有一部分后悔那样做？

turns.en: Have I in this battle to sort of counter the woke mind virus? Yeah, I guess if you consider fighting the woke mind virus, which I consider to be a civilizational threat, to be political, then yes.
turns.zh: 我在这场战斗中是为了对抗觉醒病毒？是的，我想如果你认为对抗觉醒病毒——我认为这是文明威胁——是政治性的，那么是的。

turns.en: So basically going into the battleground of politics, is there a part of you that regrets that?
turns.zh: 所以基本上进入政治战场，你有没有一部分后悔？

turns.en: Yes, I don't know if this is necessarily sort of one candidate or another candidate, but it's generally against things that are anti-meritocratic, or where there's an attempt to suppress discussion, where even discussing a topic is not allowed. The woke mind virus is communism rebranded.
turns.zh: 是的，我不知道这一定是支持某个候选人还是另一个，但它通常反对反精英主义的东西，或者试图压制讨论的地方，甚至不允许讨论某个话题。觉醒病毒是共产主义改头换面。

turns.en: Well, I mean, that's said because of that battle against the woke mind virus, you're perceived as being right-wing. If the woke is left, then I suppose that would be true. But I'm not sure. I think there are aspects of the left that are good. I mean, if you're in favor of the environment, or if you want to have a positive future for humanity, if you believe in empathy for your fellow human beings, being kind or not cruel, whatever those values are. You said that you were previously left or center-left. What would you like to see in order for you to vote for Democrats again?
turns.zh: 嗯，我是说，正因为对抗觉醒病毒的战斗，你被视为右翼。如果觉醒是左翼，那么我想那是对的。但我不确定。我认为左翼有些方面是好的。我是说，如果你支持环境，或者你想为人类创造积极的未来，如果你相信对同胞的同情，善良或不残忍，无论那些价值观是什么。你之前说过你曾是左派或中间偏左。你希望看到什么才能再次投票给民主党？

turns.en: No, I would say that I would be probably left of center on social issues, probably a little bit right of center on economic issues, and that still holds true. Yes. But I think that's probably half the country, isn't it? Maybe more.
turns.zh: 不，我会说我在社会问题上可能偏左，在经济问题上可能稍微偏右，这仍然成立。是的。但我想那大概占全国一半，不是吗？也许更多。

turns.en: Are you and AOC secretly friends? Or bigger question, do you wish you and her, and just people in general of all political persuasions, would talk more with empathy and maybe have a little bit more fun and good vibes and humor online?
turns.zh: 你和AOC是秘密朋友吗？或者更大的问题，你希望你和她的关系，以及所有政治派别的人，能更有同理心地交谈，也许在网上多一点乐趣、好氛围和幽默？

turns.en: I'm always in favor of humor. That's why we have funny mode. But good vibes, camaraderie, humor, friendship. Yeah. I don't know AOC. I was at the Met Gala when she attended, and she was wearing this dress. But I could only see one side of it, so it looked like "Eat the Rich." I don't know what the rest of it said.
turns.zh: 我总是支持幽默。所以我们才有搞笑模式。但好氛围、友情、幽默、友谊。是的。我不认识AOC。我在Met Gala上见过她，她穿着一条裙子。但我只能看到一面，所以看起来像是“吃富人”。我不知道其余部分写了什么。

turns.en: Yeah, something not sure. Tell me about the "Eat the Rich." I think we should have a language model complete what are the possible ways to complete that sentence. And so I guess that didn't work out well. Well, there's still hope. I root for friendship. Sure, sounds good. More Carolistic.
turns.zh: 对，不太确定。跟我说说“吃富人”。我觉得我们应该让语言模型补全那个句子的可能方式。所以我想那不太顺利。好吧，还有希望。我支持友谊。当然，听起来不错。更卡罗尔式。

turns.en: You're one of, if not the most, famous, wealthy, and powerful people in the world. In your position, it's difficult to find people you can trust.
turns.zh: 你是世界上最有名、最富有、最有权势的人之一，如果不是最的话。在你的位置上，很难找到可以信任的人。

turns.en: Trust no one. Not even yourself.
turns.zh: 不要相信任何人。连自己都不要信。

turns.en: Not trusting yourself? Okay, well, you're saying that jokingly, but is there some "trust no one, not even yourself"? No one going, need an hour just to think about that, and maybe some drugs, and maybe Grok. I mean, is there some aspect to that when just existing in a world where everybody wants something from you? How hard is it to exist in that world?
turns.zh: 不信任自己？好吧，你是在开玩笑，但有没有“不要相信任何人，连自己都不要信”的成分？没人会花一个小时去想那个，也许还要点毒品，也许还有Grok。我是说，当生活在一个每个人都想从你这里得到什么的世界里，有没有一些方面？在那个世界里生存有多难？

turns.en: I'll survive. There's a song like that too, "I Will Survive." Will you petrified at first? Okay, I forget the rest of the lyrics. But is there, you don't struggle with this? I mean, I know you survive, but like, there's ways. Petrify as a spell, the Druid tree. What does it do? Petrify, it turns the monsters into stone. Oh, like literally? Yeah, for like six seconds. Well, the second there's so much math in Diablo that breaks my brain. It's like math nonstop. I mean, really, you're like laughing at it, but you don't. It can put a huge amount of tension on a mind. Yes, it can be definitely stressful at times.
turns.zh: 我会活下去。有首歌叫《我会活下去》。你一开始会石化吗？好吧，我忘了剩下的歌词。但有没有，你不为此挣扎？我是说，我知道你活下来了，但就像，有办法。石化作为法术，德鲁伊树。它做什么？石化，把怪物变成石头。哦，像字面意思？对，大概六秒。好吧，第二点，暗黑破坏神里有太多数学，让我脑子炸了。就像不停的数学。我是说，真的，你在嘲笑它，但你没有。它会给大脑带来巨大压力。是的，有时肯定很有压力。

turns.en: Well, how do you know who you can trust in work and personal life?
turns.zh: 那么，你怎么知道在工作和个人生活中可以信任谁？

turns.en: I mean, I guess you look at somebody's track record over time. And if they've got a, you know, I guess you kind of use your neural net to assess someone. Neural nets don't feel pain. Your neural net has consciousness, it might feel pain when people betray you. Can make, I mean, to be frank, I've almost never been betrayed. It's very, very rare. So you know, for what it's worth, I guess karma, be good to people and they'll be good to you. Yeah, karma is real.
turns.zh: 我是说，我想你会看一个人长期的表现。如果他们有一个，你知道，我想你有点像用你的神经网络来评估一个人。神经网络不会感到痛苦。你的神经网络有意识，当人们背叛你时，它可能会感到痛苦。可以让人，我是说，坦白说，我几乎从未被背叛过。非常非常罕见。所以你知道，不管怎样，我想因果报应，对人好，他们也会对你好。是的，因果报应是真实的。

turns.en: Are there people you trust? Let me edit that question. Are there people close to you that call you odd? And your, well, the expert form is very helpful for that if you're looking for critical feedback. Can it push you into the extremes more, the extremes of thought, make you cynical about human nature in general?
turns.zh: 有没有你信任的人？让我修改一下这个问题。有没有亲近的人说你古怪？还有你的，嗯，专家形式对此很有帮助，如果你在寻找批评性反馈。它会不会把你推向更极端，思想的极端，让你对人性普遍变得愤世嫉俗？

turns.en: I don't think I will be.
turns.zh: 我不认为我会。

turns.en: In fact, I think one should never trust a cynic. The reason is that cynics excuse their own bad behavior by saying everyone does it. So I always see it as a red flag if someone is a true cynic. There's a degree of projection there, which is fun to watch from the outside and enjoy the hypocrisy. But this is an important point: if somebody is cynical, meaning they see bad behavior in everyone, it's easy for them to excuse their own bad behavior by saying everyone does it. That's not true. Most people are kind of medium good. I do wish people on X would be better at seeing the good in others' behavior. There seems to be a bias towards seeing the negative; the negative is sexier, more viral. I don't know what that is about human nature.
turns.zh: 事实上，我认为永远不要相信一个愤世嫉俗的人。原因是愤世嫉俗者会以“大家都这么做”来为自己的不良行为开脱。所以如果有人是真正的愤世嫉俗者，我会认为这是一个危险信号。这其中有一定程度的投射，从外部看这种虚伪很有趣。但有一点很重要：如果一个人愤世嫉俗，认为所有人都有不良行为，他们就很容易用“大家都这么做”来为自己的行为开脱。这不是真的。大多数人都是中等偏好的。我希望X平台上的人能更善于看到他人行为中的优点。似乎存在一种偏向负面的倾向；负面信息更性感、更容易传播。我不确定这是人性的哪一部分。

turns.en: I find the X platform to be less negative than legacy media. If you read a conventional newspaper, it just makes you sad. Whereas on X, I really get more laughs per day than everything else combined.
turns.zh: 我发现X平台比传统媒体负面情绪更少。如果你读传统报纸，只会让你难过。而在X上，我每天得到的笑声比所有其他东西加起来还多。

turns.en: Laughs are one thing, but they don't perfectly overlap with good vibes and support, like celebrating others in an awesome way. It feels that is outweighed by negativity. But it's better than mainstream media, which is almost relentlessly negative about everything. Conventional news tries to answer: what is the worst thing that happened on Earth today? And it's a big world, so on any given day something bad has happened. There's just a strong negative bias in the news. A possible explanation is evolutionary: bad news historically could be fatal, like a lion or an enemy tribe. Good news, like finding berries, is nice but not essential.
turns.zh: 笑是一回事，但它与积极氛围和支持并不完全重叠，比如以很棒的方式庆祝他人。感觉负面情绪占了上风。但这比主流媒体好，主流媒体几乎对一切都无情地负面。传统新闻试图回答：今天地球上发生了什么最糟糕的事？世界很大，所以每天都有坏事发生。新闻中就是有强烈的负面偏见。一个可能的解释是进化：历史上坏消息可能是致命的，比如狮子或敌对部落。好消息，比如找到浆果，虽然好但不是必需的。

turns.en: Our old friend Tesla Autopilot is probably one of the most intelligent real-world AI systems in the world. You followed it from the beginning. It was one of the most incredible robots and continues to be. It was exciting when it generalized from a robot on wheels to a real-world AI system that perceives the world and can have different embodiments.
turns.zh: 我们的老朋友特斯拉自动驾驶可能是世界上最智能的现实世界AI系统之一。你从一开始就关注它。它一直是最不可思议的机器人之一。当它从轮式机器人泛化为一个感知世界并可能拥有不同形态的现实世界AI系统时，非常令人兴奋。

turns.en: The really wild thing about the end-to-end training is that it learns to read, like you can read science, but we never taught it to read. We never taught it what a car was, or a person, or a bicyclist. It learned what all those objects on the road are from video, just from watching videos. Just like humans: photons in, controls out. The vast majority of information reaching our brain is from our eyes. The output is our motor signals to our fingers and mouth. Photons in, controls out. The same is true of the car. By looking at the sequence of images, it finds correlative clusters and understands something deep about the world. It's beautiful. That's how our brain works. The question is how far can it go? There seems to be a deep similarity between what LLMs do with self-supervised learning on text and what Tesla Autopilot does. They are both headed towards AGI, but Tesla's approach is much more compute-efficient because it had to be. We only have 100 watts and a brain computer of 144 trillion operations per second. It's understanding the world with only 256 values. The path to AGI through real-world understanding might have more significant impact because it will understand the real world faster than LLMs and integrate with humans faster. Tesla's approach is fundamentally more compute-efficient. Our brain is very compute-efficient and energy-efficient. The thinking part of our brain is less than 10 watts, yet it can produce a much better novel than a 10-megawatt GPU cluster. That's a six orders of magnitude difference. AI has gotten to where it is via brute force, but that's not where it will end up. Over time, models will get smaller and produce sensible output with far less compute and power. Tesla is arguably ahead on that front because we've been forced to understand the world with 100 watts of compute. We fixed a bunch of fundamental functions with Hardware 3 and Hardware 4, and Hardware 5 will be even better. It appears that the car will be able to drive better than a human even with Hardware 3 and 100 watts, and with optimization, probably less than 50 watts.
turns.zh: 端到端训练真正神奇的地方在于它学会了阅读，比如你可以阅读科学文章，但我们从未教过它阅读。我们从未教过它什么是汽车、人或骑自行车的人。它从视频中学会了路上所有物体是什么，仅仅通过观看视频。就像人类一样：光子输入，控制输出。到达大脑的绝大多数信息来自眼睛。输出是我们手指和嘴巴的运动信号。光子输入，控制输出。汽车也是如此。通过观察图像序列，它找到相关聚类并理解世界的深层含义。这很美。我们的大脑就是这样工作的。问题是它能走多远？LLM在文本上进行自监督学习与特斯拉自动驾驶似乎有深刻的相似性。它们都朝着AGI发展，但特斯拉的方法在计算效率上高得多，因为它不得不如此。我们只有100瓦特和每秒144万亿次运算的大脑计算机。它只用256个值理解世界。通过现实世界理解走向AGI的路径可能影响更大，因为它会比LLM更快地理解现实世界并更快地与人类融合。特斯拉的方法从根本上更高效。我们的大脑非常高效节能。大脑的思考部分不到10瓦特，却能写出比10兆瓦GPU集群更好的小说。这是六个数量级的差异。AI通过蛮力达到现在的水平，但这不会是终点。随着时间的推移，模型会变小，用更少的算力和功耗产生合理的输出。特斯拉在这方面可以说领先，因为我们被迫用100瓦特的算力理解世界。我们用硬件3和硬件4修复了许多基本功能，硬件5会更好。看起来即使使用硬件3和100瓦特，汽车也能比人类驾驶得更好，经过优化可能不到50瓦特。

transcript.secZh: 特斯拉自动驾驶作为现实AI

transcript.sec: Lessons from Optimus and Humanoid Robotics

turns.en: What have you learned about developing Optimus and applying this real-world AI to robotic manipulation and humanoid robotics?
turns.zh: 你在开发Optimus以及将这种现实世界AI应用于机器人操作和人形机器人方面学到了什么？

transcript.secZh: 来自Optimus与人形机器人的教训

turns.en: Some interesting tiny or big things you've understood. I was surprised at the fact that we had to develop every part of the robot ourselves, that there were no off-the-shelf motors, electronics, sensors. Like we had to develop everything.
turns.zh: 你理解到的一些有趣的小事或大事。我惊讶于我们必须自己开发机器人的每一个部件，没有现成的电机、电子设备、传感器。我们不得不开发一切。

turns.en: We couldn't actually find a source of electric motors for any amount of money. So it's not even just efficient or inexpensive, it's like anything. There's nothing. Actually, everything has to be designed from scratch. We tried hard to find anything that was... because you think of how many electric motors are made in the world, there are tens of thousands, hundreds of thousands of electric motor designs. None of them were suitable for a humanoid robot, literally none. So we had to develop our own design, design it specifically for what a humanoid robot needs.
turns.zh: 我们实际上根本找不到任何价位的电机来源。所以这不仅仅是效率或成本的问题，而是根本没有。实际上，一切都必须从头设计。我们努力寻找任何现成的东西，因为你想世界上制造了多少电机，有成千上万种电机设计。但没有一种适合人形机器人，一个都没有。所以我们不得不开发自己的设计，专门针对人形机器人的需求来设计。

turns.en: How hard was it to design something that can be mass manufactured, could be relatively inexpensive? I mean, if you compare to Boston Dynamics Atlas, it's a very expensive robot.
turns.zh: 设计一个可以大规模生产、相对便宜的机器人有多难？我的意思是，如果和波士顿动力的 Atlas 相比，它非常昂贵。

turns.en: It is designed to be manufactured in the same way that we would make a car. And I think ultimately we can make Optimus for less than the cost of a car. It should be, because if you look at the mass of the robot, it's much smaller, and the car has many actuators in it. The car has more actuators than the robot, but the actuators are kind of interesting in a humanoid robot with fingers. So Optimus has really nice hands and fingers, and they can do some interesting manipulation, soft touch robotics.
turns.zh: 它的设计是为了像制造汽车一样进行生产。我认为最终我们可以让 Optimus 的成本低于一辆汽车。应该如此，因为你看机器人的质量要小得多，而汽车有很多执行器。汽车的执行器比机器人多，但人形机器人手指的执行器很有意思。所以 Optimus 有非常灵巧的手和手指，它们可以进行一些有趣的操作，比如软触机器人技术。

turns.en: One of the tests, goals I have, is can it pick up a needle and a thread and thread the needle just by looking? How far away are we from that? Just by looking.
turns.zh: 我的一个测试目标，是它能不能拿起针和线，仅通过视觉穿针引线？我们离那还有多远？仅通过视觉。

turns.en: Maybe a year. Although I go back to, I'm optimistic on time. The work that we're doing in the car will translate to the robot. The perception, or also the control. No, the controls are different, but the video in, controls out. The car is a robot on four wheels. The Optimus is a robot with hands and legs. So they're very similar. So the entire machinery of the learning process, you just have a different set of controls. Optimus will figure out how to do things by watching videos.
turns.zh: 也许一年。尽管我回过头来，我对时间持乐观态度。我们在汽车上做的工作会迁移到机器人上。感知，或者控制。不，控制不同，但视频输入、控制输出。汽车是四个轮子的机器人。Optimus 是有手有脚的机器人。所以它们非常相似。所以整个学习过程的机制，你只是有一套不同的控制。Optimus 会通过观看视频来学会做事。

turns.en: As the saying goes, 'Be kind, for everyone you meet is fighting a battle you know nothing about.' It's true. What's something difficult you're going through that people don't often see?
turns.zh: 俗话说，‘善待他人，因为你遇到的每个人都在打一场你一无所知的仗。’这是真的。你正在经历什么人们通常看不到的困难？

turns.en: Trying to feed Uber? No, I mean, you know, my mind is a storm, and I don't think most people would want to be me. They may think they would want to be me, but they don't know, they don't understand.
turns.zh: 试图喂饱 Uber？不，我是说，我的思绪像风暴一样，我不认为大多数人想成为我。他们可能以为他们想成为我，但他们不知道，他们不理解。

turns.en: How are you doing? I mean, overall okay?
turns.zh: 你怎么样？我是说，总体上还好吗？

turns.en: In the grand scheme of things, I can't complain.
turns.zh: 从大局来看，我没什么可抱怨的。

turns.en: Do you get lonely sometimes?
turns.zh: 你有时会感到孤独吗？

turns.en: But my kids and friends keep me company, so not existential. But there are many nights I sleep alone. I don't have to, but I do.
turns.zh: 但我的孩子和朋友陪伴着我，所以不是存在性的孤独。但有很多夜晚我独自入睡。我不必这样，但我确实如此。

turns.en: Walter Isaacson in his new biography of you wrote about your difficult childhood. Will you ever find forgiveness in your heart for everything that has happened to you in that period of your life?
turns.zh: 沃尔特·艾萨克森在你新的传记中写到了你艰难的童年。你会在心中原谅你生命中那段时期发生的一切吗？

turns.en: What is forgiveness? I do not, at least I don't think I have a resentment, so nothing to forgive. You know, forgiveness is difficult for people. It seems like you don't harbor the resentment.
turns.zh: 什么是原谅？我没有，至少我不认为我有怨恨，所以没什么可原谅的。你知道，原谅对人们来说很难。看起来你没有心怀怨恨。

turns.en: I mean, I try to think about what is going to affect the future in a good way, and holding on to grudges does not affect the future in a good way.
turns.zh: 我的意思是，我试着思考什么会以好的方式影响未来，而心怀怨恨不会以好的方式影响未来。

turns.en: You're a father, a proud father. What have you learned about life from your kids?
turns.zh: 你是一位父亲，一位自豪的父亲。你从你的孩子身上学到了什么关于生活的东西？

turns.en: Those little biological organisms. I mean, developing AI and watching, say, a little X grow is fascinating, because there are far more parallels than I would have expected. I mean, I can see his biological neural net making more and more sense of the world, and I can see the digital neural net making more and more sense of the world at the same time.
turns.zh: 那些小的生物有机体。我的意思是，开发 AI 和看着，比如说，小 X 成长是迷人的，因为相似之处比我预期的要多得多。我是说，我可以看到他的生物神经网络越来越理解世界，同时我也看到数字神经网络越来越理解世界。

turns.en: Do you see the beauty and magic in both?
turns.zh: 你在两者中都看到了美和魔力吗？

turns.en: Yes. I mean, one of the things with kids is that you kind of see the world anew in their eyes. To them, everything is new and fresh, and when you see them experience the world, the world is new and fresh to you too.
turns.zh: 是的。我的意思是，和孩子在一起的一件事是，你通过他们的眼睛重新看待世界。对他们来说，一切都是新鲜和崭新的，当你看到他们体验世界时，世界对你来说也是新鲜和崭新的。

turns.en: Well, Elon, I just want to say thank you for your kindness to me and friendship over the years, for seeing something in a silly kid like me, as you've done for many others, and thank you for having hope for a positive future for humanity and for working your ass off to make it happen. Thank you.
turns.zh: 好了，埃隆，我只想说感谢你多年来对我的善意和友谊，感谢你在我这样一个傻孩子身上看到了什么，就像你为许多其他人所做的那样，感谢你对人类积极未来抱有希望，并为此拼命工作。谢谢。

turns.en: Thanks, Lex.
turns.zh: 谢谢，莱克斯。

turns.en: Thank you for listening to this conversation with Elon Musk. To support this podcast, please check out our sponsors in the description. And now let me leave you with some words that Walter Isaacson wrote about the central philosophy of how Elon approaches difficult problems: 'The only rules are the ones dictated by the laws of physics.' Thank you for listening, and hope to see you next time.
turns.zh: 感谢收听与埃隆·马斯克的这次对话。要支持本播客，请查看描述中的赞助商。现在让我用沃尔特·艾萨克森写的关于埃隆处理困难问题的核心哲学的话来结束：‘唯一的规则是由物理定律决定的规则。’感谢收听，希望下次再见。
